zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Nicholas Rishton於1445年12月出生在英國藍卡爾利申頓時，他的父親Henry Rishton20歲，母親Agnes Sherburne21歲。他於大約1475年與Margaret Radcliffe在英國藍卡爾登肯哈爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1508年5月3日在英國英格蘭藍卡爾享年62歲。

nameMeanings.name: Rishton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Rushton  . Early bearers: Aleander Rishton, 1560 in   IGI   (Great Harwood, Lancs); Anna Rishton, 1598, Elizabeth Rushton, 1635 in   IGI   (Altham, Lancs); Robert Rishton, 1603, James Rushton, 1627, Robert Riston, 1757 in   IGI   (Colne, Lancs).

variants.name: Rushton
meanings: English: habitational name from Rishton (Lancashire) and Rushton (Staffordshire), and perhaps also from Rushton (Cheshire, Northamptonshire), Rushton in East Stoke (Dorset), and Rhiston (Shropshire). The placenames derive from Old English risc ‘rush’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: IGI
meanings: English: perhaps an altered form of   Pritchett   or   Pritchard  . Compare Katherine Pratchett, 1654, Janam Pritchett, 1675 in   IGI   (Acton by Nantwich, Cheshire); Gulielmus Pritchard, 1675, Mary Pratchett, 1698 in   IGI   (Hatfield, Herts).

variants.name: Riston
meanings: English: habitational name from Long Riston (East Yorkshire), North and South Reston (Lincolnshire), and East and Sco Ruston (Norfolk). The placenames derive from Old English hrīs ‘brushwood’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The name may sometimes have been confused with Ritson .
Scottish: habitational name from Reston in Coldingham (Berwickshire), cognate with 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Nicholas Rishton was born in December 1445, in Rishton, Lancashire, England, his father, Henry Rishton, was 20 and his mother, Agnes Sherburne, was 21. He married Margaret Radcliffe about 1475, in Dunkenhalgh, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 3 May 1508, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62.

Il a épousé Margaret Radcliffe environ 1475, à Dunkenhalgh, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 3 mai 1508, à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Rishton(이)가 1445년 12월 영국 랭카실르 리슈턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Rishton(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Sherburne(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1475년 영국 랭카실르 던켄할그h에서 Margaret Radcliffe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1508년 5월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.
